source: Invest Like the Best (YouTube) — Watts and wafers
summary: Capitalism will solve the power/watts shortage for AI data centers by 2027-2028, with orbital compute as the longer-term solution
summary_extended: Baker argues that the primary bottleneck for AI data center buildout has shifted from chips or energy supply to zoning and regulatory approvals, and that capitalism will resolve the watts shortage by 2027-2028 as turbine manufacturing capacity ramps and alternative energy sources come online. He bases this on direct time spent at SpaceX's Starbase facility with engineers who are highly confident in solving the technical challenges of orbital compute — racks in space cooled and connected by lasers — as the longer-term structural solution to power constraints. The investment implication is that power generation and grid infrastructure companies stand to benefit in the medium term as data center buildout accelerates once regulatory bottlenecks clear, while SpaceX (private) is the primary vehicle for the orbital compute thesis. Baker cites a specific timeline of 2027-2028 for the watts shortage to begin alleviating, suggesting near-term power scarcity remains a tailwind for existing clean power producers with contracted capacity.
detail: Baker believes zoning and regulatory approval — not energy or chips — are now the primary gating factors for data center buildout. He expects the watts shortage to begin alleviating in 2027-2028 as turbine capacity ramps and alternative energy sources come online. Orbital compute (racks in space connected by lasers) is the longer-term solution, with SpaceX as the primary vehicle.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this as a structural view with specific timeline (27-28) and has spent significant time at Starbase with SpaceX engineers who are 'very confident' on the cooling/technical challenges
quote_fragment: watts shortage will probably begin to alleviate 27, 28

ticker_audit.reasoning: GE Vernova is a major turbine manufacturer; Baker specifically cites turbine capacity ramping as a key mechanism for alleviating the watts shortage, making GEV a direct beneficiary of this thesis.

Vistra is a power producer with significant capacity that benefits from sustained high power demand from AI data centers during the 2025-2027 scarcity window Baker describes. Constellation Energy is a clean power producer with nuclear capacity contracted to data centers; it directly benefits from the power scarcity thesis Baker outlines prior to the 2027-2028 alleviation.
